title: daphne mezereum f. plena
intro: Daphne mezereum f. plena, also known as the flowering or winter daphne, is a flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is a shrub-like perennial that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is valued for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant contains daphnin, a toxic compound with medicinal properties, which has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. Daphne mezereum f. plena can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and has been traditionally used to reduce pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and support immune function.
